explicit_text: I was born and raised in St. Louis, Missouri, a city rich with history and family roots. My family’s optical business was based downtown, and I grew up surrounded by the culture and traditions of this city. St. Louis was also the site of the 1904 World's Fair, an event my family was connected to through their photography business. Growing up here shaped much of my education and early life experiences.
implicit_text: I was born and raised in a Midwestern city known for its rich history and family roots. My family’s optical business was based downtown, and I grew up surrounded by the culture and traditions of this place. This city was also the site of the 1904 World's Fair, an event my family was connected to through their photography business. Growing up here shaped much of my education and early life experiences.

explicit_text: My mother attended Webster College, which is now known as Webster University, way back in her youth. She was a gracious lady with a talent for decorating, and her education there was part of her foundation. Although I didn’t attend, Webster University was part of my family’s educational background in St. Louis.
implicit_text: My mother attended a private college in the same city, which later changed its name to a university, way back in her youth. She was a gracious lady with a talent for decorating, and her education there was part of her foundation. Although I didn’t attend, that university was part of my family’s educational background in the city.

explicit_text: Erker Brothers Optical Company was my family’s business, started by my grandfather and great-uncle in St. Louis. They sold eyeglasses, radios, weather instruments, and even were official photographers for the 1904 World's Fair. My father and brothers expanded the business, which lasted for generations, and I even worked there grinding lenses when I was young. Despite family disputes later on, the company remains a significant part of my family’s legacy.

explicit_text: The 1904 World's Fair in St. Louis was a major event that my family was involved with through their optical and photography business. Erker Brothers Optical Company served as official photographers for the fair, which is a point of pride and historical significance for us. I have books and postcards from that era that bear our company’s name, connecting our family history to this landmark event.

explicit_text: I attended Our Lady of Lourdes School for my grade school education, just like my brothers and sisters. It was a Catholic school in St. Louis where I was baptized by a pastor who was born in 1868, which always amazed me. This school laid the foundation for my complete Catholic education before I moved on to high school.
implicit_text: I attended a Catholic grade school in the city for my early education, just like my brothers and sisters. I was baptized there by a pastor born in 1868, which always amazed me. This school laid the foundation for my complete Catholic education before I moved on to high school.

explicit_text: I graduated from St. Louis University High School, the oldest high school west of the Mississippi River, founded in 1818. I was active there as business manager of the yearbook, worked on the newspaper, and was on the swimming team. My father worked hard to pay the tuition, which was quite expensive at the time, and I have fond memories of my time there.

explicit_text: I spent several years studying at Kenrick Seminary, now known as Cardinal Glennon College, with the intention of becoming a priest. I was fully committed to the vocation and lived a cloistered life there. However, after some youthful indiscretions, including sneaking out to see a movie and bowling late at night, I was asked to take a sabbatical and ultimately did not complete my studies.

explicit_text: A Summer Place was the movie some of us at Kenrick Seminary tried to sneak out to see at the High Point Theater. Unfortunately, when we arrived, the movie wasn’t playing yet, so we ended up going to a bar and then bowling at Tropicana Lanes instead. I never actually saw A Summer Place, and it became a memorable part of my seminary days for all the wrong reasons.

We never made it to the film, but her name is forever linked to that night of mischief and youthful rebellion. I’ve never seen the movie since, but Sandra Dee’s role is part of that story.

The theater is part of a memorable story from my youth involving sneaking out and getting into trouble.

We spent the night bowling and goofing off before sneaking back to Kenrick Seminary in time for morning prayers. Tropicana Lanes was part of that memorable night of youthful mischief during my seminary years.

explicit_text: Saint Louis University played a role in my story during my time at Kenrick Seminary because some of my friends were studying there. The Jesuits at Saint Louis University got wind of our sneaking out and conducted an investigation that eventually led to us being confronted by seminary authorities. The university and its Jesuit faculty were part of the disciplinary process that influenced my path.

They took the matter seriously and helped uncover who was involved, which led to disciplinary actions. I had some respect for them, even though it was a tough lesson during my seminary years.

Their presence was part of the context during my seminary years and the events that unfolded.
